rationale: Entire 6.3-year career spent at Cognizant and Wipro (IT services only) in DevOps/Cloud roles with no production ML, no IR/search experience, and AI exposure is limited to self-study courses and a small RAG side project.

Career is entirely non-technical (Content Writer, Graphic Designer, HR Manager) with roles at fictional companies (Pied Piper, Stark Industries) and no engineering or ML production experience whatsoever.

Profile is a marketing/sales career with roles at fictional companies (Dunder Mifflin, Initech) and no software engineering or ML background; skills like YOLO and Spark appear without supporting technical experience.
Entire career spans HR Manager and Marketing Manager roles at Infosys and fictional companies (Pied Piper), with no engineering, ML, or IR experience of any kind.

Backend/.NET developer at HCL (IT services) and Dunder Mifflin (fictional company) with no production ML or retrieval experience; AI exposure is limited to a self-described side RAG project and online courses.

14-year career entirely in Marketing Manager and Sales Executive roles at Wayne Enterprises and Dunder Mifflin (fictional companies), with no software engineering or ML work despite AI-themed keywords in skills.
Only 2.2 years of experience in HR Manager and Graphic Designer roles at Wipro and Initech (fictional company), with no technical or ML engineering background at all.

5.7 years of applied ML experience including production recommendation systems at BYJU's and ML engineering at Zoho, with BM25 skill and computer vision depth, but limited explicit IR/ranking or embedding-based retrieval production experience.

8.4 years spent entirely at Capgemini (IT services) in Java/software engineering roles with no production ML or IR experience; AI exposure is self-study only despite a PhD in Machine Learning from SRM.

9.1-year career is primarily frontend/full-stack development at Acme Corp and DevOps at Mphasis (IT services) with no production ML or retrieval work; MLOps skill is listed with high endorsements but the actual roles do not support it.
